channel.title: Posts on Berke Aslan
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Berke Aslan

item.title: DSA: Arrays - Part 1
item.description: Data structures and algorithms (DSA) are of great importance when solving problems. It is, however, a widely known phenomenon that a lot of CS students don’t have their DSA knowledge ready to throw against any problem they encounter. In this 3-part DSA series about arrays, we will be exploring various sorting algorithms for arrays. In part 1, we will be exploring the bubble, selection, and insertion sort algorithms. The goal of these notes is to quickly explain how an algorithm works and develop some intuition. The code is written in C, which I believe is a better language for learning DSA since you have fewer abstractions than languages like Python. This leads to a deeper understanding of the algorithms discussed and shows some of the deficiencies that naturally arise in a language like Python.

item.title: My First Post
item.description: Hey! This will be the first post on my new website. As you have probably read on the landing page, this website will primarily be used for showcasing my ML learning notes. Next to that, I hope to publish some other posts with a heavy focus on STEM. This can be anything really: from data structures and algorithms in Python (or C), to proofs of convergence of the conjugate gradient method. Nothing is out of the ordinary.
